# tg_container_state (Resource)

Manage the enabled state of a container. This resource allows you to control whether a container is running or stopped, independent of the container configuration.

This resource is useful when you want to manage container state separately from the container definition itself. For example, you might define a container in one Terraform module and control its enabled state in another.

## Example Usage

### Enable a container on a node

```terraform
resource "tg_container_state" "my_container" {
  node_id      = "35ee5516-c6d5-409b-b1ba-6aa2d0dd92fc"
  container_id = "a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890"
  enabled      = true
}
```

### Disable a container on a cluster

```terraform
resource "tg_container_state" "cluster_container" {
  cluster_fqdn = "my-cluster.example.trustgrid.io"
  container_id = "a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890"
  enabled      = false
}
```

### Use with an existing tg_container resource

```terraform
resource "tg_container" "my_app" {
  node_id   = var.node_id
  name      = "my-app"
  exec_type = "service"

  image {
    repository = "dev.trustgrid.io/my-app"
    tag        = "latest"
  }
}

# Manage the container state separately
resource "tg_container_state" "my_app_state" {
  node_id      = var.node_id
  container_id = tg_container.my_app.id
  enabled      = var.container_enabled
}
```

## Common Use Cases

### Maintenance Mode

Disable containers during maintenance windows without modifying the container configuration:

```terraform
variable "maintenance_mode" {
  type    = bool
  default = false
}

resource "tg_container_state" "production_app" {
  node_id      = var.node_id
  container_id = var.container_id
  enabled      = !var.maintenance_mode
}
```

### Environment-specific State

Control container state based on environment:

```terraform
locals {
  is_production = var.environment == "production"
}

resource "tg_container_state" "app" {
  node_id      = var.node_id
  container_id = var.container_id
  enabled      = local.is_production
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `container_id` (String) Container ID - the UUID of the container to manage
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the container should be enabled (running) or disabled (stopped)

### Optional

- `cluster_fqdn` (String) Cluster FQDN - the fully qualified domain name of the cluster where the container runs. Exactly one of `node_id` or `cluster_fqdn` must be specified.
- `node_id` (String) Node ID - the UUID of the node where the container runs. Exactly one of `node_id` or `cluster_fqdn` must be specified.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

~> **Note:** Exactly one of `node_id` or `cluster_fqdn` must be specified.

## Behavior Notes

- **No-op on delete:** Deleting this resource does not affect the container's enabled state - it simply removes the resource from Terraform state.
- **Container must exist:** The referenced container must exist. Use explicit `depends_on` or reference the container's ID to ensure proper ordering.

## Import

Container state can be imported using the node ID (or cluster FQDN) and container ID separated by a slash:

```shell
# Import using node ID
terraform import tg_container_state.example 35ee5516-c6d5-409b-b1ba-6aa2d0dd92fc/a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890

# Import using cluster FQDN
terraform import tg_container_state.example my-cluster.example.trustgrid.io/a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890
```
